subject: Properties of Expectation
question: Suppose that the joint density of $X$ and $Y$ is given by $f_{XY}(x,y) = \frac{e^{-x/y}e^{-y}}{y}$, $x,y > 0$. Compute $E(X|Y=y)$.
explanation: The conditional density is found as follows $$f_{X|Y}(x|y) = \frac{f_{XY}(x,y)}{f_Y(y)} = \frac{f_{XY}(x,y)}{\int_{-\infty}^\infty f_{XY}(x,y)\,dx} = \frac{(1/y)e^{-x/y}e^{-y}}{\int_0^\infty (1/y)e^{-x/y}e^{-y}\,dx} = \frac{(1/y)e^{-x/y}}{\int_0^\infty (1/y)e^{-x/y}\,dx} = \frac{1}{y}e^{-x/y}$$ Hence, $$E(X|Y=y) = \int_0^\infty \frac{x}{y}e^{-x/y}\,dx = -\left[ xe^{-x/y}\Big|_0^\infty - \int_0^\infty e^{-x/y}\,dx \right] = -\left[ xe^{-x/y} + ye^{-x/y} \right]_0^\infty = y$$
